tokens: Van Serg looks like a blocky-rim, fresh impact crater right now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about scuffing your feet and seeing if it looks orange underneath?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Slight differences...(Stops to listen to Bob) Don't worry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Gene, before you go away, we'd like the rest of the Rover readouts, like batteries.
And how about a SEP temp readout before one of you guys leaves there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Can you get that (SEP) on that side, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We have it, and I'm sure that Ed (
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll dust it if you can't read it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (I've) wiped the dust off (the SEP) just over the gauge.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, everything is really bad now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See if you can straighten it out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's just a sample of the kind of dust we would have got, Jack, if we hadn't of had that fender yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you get a (lens) dustbrush, and let's check our cameras.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Stay where you are, and I'll give you a zap-a-reen-o, wherever you are.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, how many bags do I have.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know, but I've got a lot of dusting to do here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you have a lot of bags?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I must have...I've got four of them is all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'd better change my bag (that is, get a new pack of sample bags to hang on his camera).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I have an empty bag (SCB) on me now, right?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't know how much time do we have here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're looking at a nominal Station 9 here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No such thing as a nominal station any more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This may be the first and only one of the traverse.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The geology won't let it be nominal.
Okay, I've got some new (sample) bags, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I guess I'm pretty good on film.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And you're going to get a radial sample here...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...and so you might check your Rover sample(r) (the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And you might give me frame count or check it to make sure you're okay.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Calling to Houston) How do you want the SEP blankets?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We been riding with this thing off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Doesn't seem like it'd get much data that way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess they're worried about getting it so hot it...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It shuts itself off when it gets above 108, so it's no good anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's been off all day, since Station 6.
We've been hoping that it would cool down so that we could get some more data, but it's not, obviously.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's not going to make it, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Changing subjects) This is starting to look like a (U.S.) Geological Survey expedition.
The vehicles are all covered with dust.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Long Pause) I don't think I can read that unless I dust it with a
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, can I get by you here?
(Does) my bag (that is, his SCB) look all right to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What are we going to do here?
(Consulting checklist) We're going to go up there and sample on the rim; look at the walls and the floor, and miscellaneous, and...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, we are on the rim (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...then you're going to take 500 millimeters when you get back to the Rover while I do a radial sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But the first thing we do is go up to the crater.
Bob, I think the mantle objective here really is immaterial, because the blocky ejecta around the crater covers...Oh, boy...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Stepping back from the Rover to look around) Well, it looks like it extends several hundred meters out from the rim.
Say a couple of hundred meters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're pretty close to the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll go up on the rim, Gene, and see what we've got.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me see; anything else you want me to do while I'm here (at the Rover)?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sure look like shocked rocks to me.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Lot of glass splattered on some of these, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) We might even find some shatter cones.
(Pause) Well, I'll say one thing for old Van Serg: it's blocky.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This is about...I think this is the only clearly...(Pause) Well, I won't even say that.
This is at least a large, blocky-rim crater.
But even it has the mantle dust material covering the rim, partially burying the rocks.
And it's down on the floor, as near as I can tell, and on the walls.
The crater itself has a central mound of blocks that's probably 50 meters in diameter - that's a little high - 30 meters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...intensely shattered in that area, as (are) the ones that are on the walls.
I don't see any sign of organization to the blocks in the walls, right now.
There's a possibility that, on the west wall, there's an indication that there's slightly darker gray rocks starting about halfway down the crater.
And that level is coincident with what appears to be a bench on the northwest wall.
And hints of that bench - it's not continuous - but hints of it are around on the north wall and, I think, right below us.
(Pause) The rocks are pretty badly broken in many cases.
Well, I haven't seen any real glass yet.
(Pause) We'll start looking at them a little more carefully.
Some of them...That looks like a breccia right there in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's some interesting patterns on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Turning toward the Rover) (I'd better take a) "locator".
Afraid I haven't been doing my duty on "locators", occasionally.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Gene's tearing apart one of the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it comes off in small flakes.
Let's get this one, because this will be the best oriented one for documentation.
Plus, why don't you get that one you've got inside there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Holding a sample in his scoop) Got a bag?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bag 568 is a fragment from the surface.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's a corner, I think, off the block that Gene documented here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) We'll get another sample that'll be from inside the block.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (I can) get it with this (the tongs) real easy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here's a whole big...We ought to take that just as is.
(Pause) Well, put a bag around one end if we can.
Here, the other end is smaller.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me hold it, and you put the bag on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See the white fragments in there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's got a lot of very small...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks like this big one over here.
(To Bob) You know, it might be that these might be pieces of the projectile.
'Cause it doesn't look like...It's not subfloor.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you put it end down, it may stay in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's 480, and it's a relatively tabular shape, and it's about...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) And it's going to ....
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, you did it with your hands there.
I call that "being friable", compared to what we've seen anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and let me get an "after" or two.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get a soil right over here.
(Pause; having difficulty with the scoop head again) Okay.
The soil next to the boulder, down to about 3 centimeters, is in bag 569.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) And the soil and chips about two-thirds of a meter from the boulder...(To Gene) Get another one?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) Let me get over here.
(Pause) You're going to step on your gnomon there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I wouldn't step on my gnomon.
I'm going to get this one crimped.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) There, very clearly, is a central mound.
And now that we've looked at this one, the mound looks like it's composed of gray fragment breccias much like what we've just sampled...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And again it might be related...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Finishing his prior thought)...related to the projectile.
Now, we've got to see if there is subfloor up here, or whether we're dealing with another unit somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, the more coherent rocks...This looks like subfloor.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't see any orange material either.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This particular rock we've sampled has tabular fractures (that is, a set of parallel fractures), and in one-half of the rock, they are definitely oriented.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you, I don't...(Pause) (To Bob) There's more dust on these rocks.
It's harder to see a fresh surface.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And even the floor of the crater is mantled down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That seems like a...what you got?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I think it is glass.
Houston, I've got an undocumented sample.
It's about 2 meters west of where we just sampled.
It's a glass-covered, oh, baseball-size rock in 571.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: A lot of these blocks up here, Houston - particularly the more fractured ones, but even some that aren't - are a gray-matrix, fragment (means 'clast') breccia.
And it looks like, really, the fragments are quite fine.
On the rim anyway, we haven't seen any large fragments.
The largest I've seen is about 2 centimeters.
But down in the (central) mound you can see some fragments that are probably half a meter in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Having joined Jack) Jack, are you going around that rim of the crater up there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I was just looking at rocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to get a pan before I leave back there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We need to see if we can get some of the subfloor.
I'm not sure I understand what's happened here, yet.
This should have brought up subfloor according to the theory, and it hasn't.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That looks like some of the...Look at some of the breccias, the blue breccias with the big old slabby white...(Look at) the fracture face with the white inclusions.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, down in the floor itself.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, it has that appearance all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...that rock that's fractured in sort of a pyramid shape down there?
The right end of the floor down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's a unique fracture, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And there's another one that's fractured almost in a...(Stops to listen to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...And we'd like to be moving from here in about 10 minutes, so we probably better be trending back toward the Rover, unless you're seeing something really great out there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well...Hey, Bob, we ought to find out whether or not...(We) ought to find out what the rock is here, if you've got a little time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, let me to put this in your bag and start...I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm just telling you to start thinking about getting back.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: One thing I noticed we do uncover.
There's a lot of, oh, 2-, 3-, 4-millimeter-size fragments of glass we're kicking up all over the place.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Moving southeast along the rim crest) Hey, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think there's some subfloor here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to try to document it.
But I tell you, most of the rocks are the fine-fragment breccias.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me see if I can't get one of those little...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Stopping about 4 meters northwest of Jack) You see, if they're like Pele's...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we can get some over here.
If you're careful coming over here, we can get glass that looks like it may have crystallized (that is, solidified) in place here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm talking about those little balls, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See that...Whoo, take it easy...Take it easy.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pointing with the scoop) Yeah, but put your gnomon right over here, and we can get (indicating) that for glass and that for subfloor.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But I'm not sure that is.
Everything is covered with dust here, and it's hard to tell the types.
Most of the rocks we're seeing are breccias.
(Pause) Make sure that glass is in your stereo.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't have any bags so...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, the glass looks like a glass agglutinate.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that will survive going back (to Earth) now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's a frothy glass agglutinate; it's going to be in bag 481.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it looks almost like a cowpie-type of bomb, Bob, if you'll pardon the expression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know about anybody else.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's an aggregate of glass in...Or, it's a pile of about four fragments, much like the one we're sampling.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, we want to get a good scoop sample here.
Maybe can we get some of those little fine pieces of glass around.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And it (the agglutinate) looks like it's in place from the day it was born.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm having a hard time with this one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (I'll get) a piece of that rock right behind it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Trying to fit the rock in a sample bag) Just not going to be able to get that one in the bag, I don't think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's a rock, but it doesn't look like subfloor.
It looks like the blue-gray material we've been seeing...the breccia-type material.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think there's any difference (if the sample bag is sealed or not).
(You) might just as well throw them in my bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want a scoop out of here, though, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...17, why don't we get that scoop sample as the first sample of Jack's radial sample, 17?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I forgot you were doing that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Stowing the sample in Gene's SCB) Oh, boy.
Let's let that one be the last...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Handing Jack the other sample) Here's one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Those are the last ones that you can take (in the CDR SCB).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to get an "after" a picture here.
And I want to get a pan of this thing (the crater).
We can get a stereo pan as you start your radial sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You take the "after" from there, and I'll go over here and...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to go over behind me and take part of the stereo.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Where are you going to take your pan?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: From behind me, where we were.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Moving 15 feet south of Gene, along the rim crest) Well, I think I'll just take my radial right from here to the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just do that, and then you'll be right back at the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Planting his scoop) And I'll take my pan from here, so you...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Now skipping) Man, there's about four or five different modes of travel out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I'm out of film.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, I didn't get the rest of that crater down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I only got it (from) 12 o'clock (that is, down-Sun) and around (through north).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm going to be out of film, too, here before long.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just don't worry about it then.
Just press on with your radial samples.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a good pan over here.
Did you get the crater at all?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got the right (north) half of it and probably two-thirds of it, so I'm just going to have to let that do.
I'm going to see if I can get some 500's while you're doing that (radial sample).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, this isn't going to be an ideal radial sample; but it will have to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Singing as he heads back to the Rover, using the kangaroo or bunny hop) Hippity-hopping over hill and dale.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, would you tell me what your primary desires are again on the 500, based upon what we have?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The primary desire will be the North Massif: the blocks, and the (boulder) trails.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And while you're at the Rover, they want you to take the gravimeter off again, and we'll get another "Rover"...(Correcting himself) Well, another surface measurement here, as well, to check against the "Rover".
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think I owe you one of those, don't I?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I didn't know we were going to do both of these things.
I thought we were going to do one or the other.
But, if we're going to do it, we might as well do it right!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Taking the bag off the LRV Sampler) Okay, bag...Standby.
52 Yankee is at the rim crest.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll tell you what I'm going to do.
I'm going to use the Rover to steady the 500, and see what happens.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) Oh, I should have let you take this scoop back.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This (radial sample) isn't working out too well, Dr.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This isn't working out too well.
I've got to get rid of this scoop.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just set it there and take your sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll take the samples going back.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (At the Rover) Just like in training, the scoop doesn't stay locked to the extension (handle).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (After a great deal of discussion in Houston) Okay, 17.
We'd like you to press on.
We'd like to leave here immediately, if not sooner, to head for Station 10.
And we'll give you some information here on (film) mags.
We need the gravimeter put back on the Rover, if you haven't already.
If it's on the ground, we didn't get the mark, but it's probably done by now.
And we're going to take the DSEA (the SEP data storage unit) out of the tape recorder here, and we'd like to get that all done pronto.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: 85 is the mag count on the 500.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think that's a smart move, Bob.
I don't think the radial sample's going to tell you much here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, you ought to get a scoop of that dirt, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We don't have a scoop of it, do we?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I don't know what's underneath it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I wanted to make sure we got some of those small glass balls.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we'll get a scoop of it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Putting the gravimeter on the Rover) Up on the top.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 17, we're anxious for you guys to get going.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Here's your gravimeter reading from the surface; 670, 057, 101; 670, 057, 101.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want me to change my mag at the next station?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This may be the youngest mantle over whatever was...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Bob, we've got to take 5 more minutes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What Jack's done is, he dug a trench in a southwest-northeast direction, and he discovered - about 3 inches below, 4 inches below the surface - a very light-gray material.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm trying to get the upper portion there.
(To Bob) The first 2 centimeters (is going in) bag 483.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Spilling about half of the sample) Augh!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, you got to put that away, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) And the next 10 centimeters of the light-gray material, will be, probably, in 486.
(If I can) get it (the sample bag) off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What did I say (for the prior sample) 483, 484?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: He's mad at us now (for overstaying the station).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Softly, in a friendly tone) How'd you guess?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) Bob, a possibility here is that this upper 6 inches of gray material, in here, is the latest mantling in the area and the light-colored debris may be what's left over from the (Van Serg) impact.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But we'd like to get you going.
(Pause) In case you didn't get the clue.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, we'll change them at the next station.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I've got to have some.
I got to get some, or I can't take...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We need Jack to put on magazine Nancy (Apollo magazine 143).
And we'd like, Gene, for you to pull out the DSEA tape recorder at this station.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I need a magazine too, Bob.
I don't have any film at all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That'll be Bravo (Apollo magazine 134, which Gene used during the first EVA) if you change yours here, or you could change it at Station 10.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's just as easy while we're in there; (that is, while they have Gene's seat raised to get a magazine for Jack).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bravo was outside there; I saw it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get this...Hold it long enough for me to get this (
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't put that back in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) We lost the dark slide out of Bravo, and it's in the dirt.
I'm not going to pick it up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's no point in putting it back in.
It probably wouldn't go in anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Going to the gate) And I don't know what the mag count is, but let me get the DSEA.
If this thing is true to form, I'm going to have to get in there...I got to...(Surprised) The bow is tripped!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, now what's...Hey, we got some rocks in that big bag.
I hope there's something on it.
I can't even pick up that big bag to close the gate!
(Pause) I've got to trip that latch with tongs or something to lock it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...had a change of heart here again, as usual.
And we're going to drop Station 10 now that we've hurried you so much, and we're going to get a double core here.
And we'd like to get some football-size rocks while you're doing that.
But double core here, and then we're going to leave here and go back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You don't want a double core here.
I don't think we can do it, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Jack) You don't think we'll get through that stuff you just trenched?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm afraid there are rocks all through it, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Going to the gate) Let's try it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I don't like to try things that there's a probability of failure on, if you can...You're just going to lose some time.
Okay, mag Nancy is on the LMP's camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Looking around) Well, you can see the rock population here, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If we get a single, we get a single out of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Going around the front of the Rover to his seat) Oh, you're doing it, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Not even going to debate the issue?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It takes too much time debating it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let's see how much time it takes.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and we need a "lower" out of my bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: A "lower" out of my bag is all we need.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah I want to get (garbled) for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We have to have you guys moving in 10 minutes.
And we'd like to also deploy EP number 5 here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll start on the (garbled under Jack).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Why don't you get (seismic package number) 5 out, and I'll start on the core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll put that right there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Holding the core in front of the TV lens, fitting them together) Okay.
The lower is 50; the upper is 37.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you put it up...Well...You put the gnomon away.
Put it fairly near that trench.
At least there is some documentation there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll try to have the pan going while you're doing it.
Okay, Houston...(To Gene) Which way you going to drive out of here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we'll document it back to the Rover, I guess is the best way.
That doesn't look too hard, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Try to...Oops, looks like you proved me wrong!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The first core was easy; the second one a little tougher; and then it got tough down at the end.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Stay there, I'm getting a picture of you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (It's) full, but it wants to slide out.
It looks like just the same stuff we've been traveling through.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you better help Gene with recovering that core there where he thinks it's going to fall out.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You know, I think you're right.
And if you'll just wait until I finish the pan, that's exactly what I'm going to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I didn't know what you were doing.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I can take this one off.
And it's...Just any little movement and you'll lose some of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Uh-oh, you almost knocked some out.
Get your...you know where your thing (the rammer) is?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but I need you...The cap's on you.
The last one's gone off the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Any little movement and that stuff starts...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll get the rammer (off the back of Jack's PLSS).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Even these pins are getting stiff.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The top rammed down, oh, almost half way without any effort.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The scoop's back on (the extension handle).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The bottom rammed down about an inch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Turn around and I'll get this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What was the last thing - let's see - we had to do?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I got one sample of a radial sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughing) (And the sample is) in my pocket.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And have we got the gravimeter back on the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we want to get a large block.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, let's get a couple of them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger; but we have to have the wheels rolling...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I don't know if we can do that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We want the wheels rolling in 4 minutes, so I don't think it's practical at this time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, we cannot get an SESC in 4 minutes...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, I've got to push this latch on the gate...(correcting himself) on the pallet to get it locked.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Push the pallet while I trip the latch, will you?
Because I got to trip the latch.
There's so much dust in that core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now...Now that's where...Now let me trip it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Got a big rock there, too?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you know, the thing that amazes me is that there's no subfloor around here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm about ready to clean up the Rover here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What's out there in the distance on a hillside in the field-of-view of the camera?
The camera is pointing at it.
(Pause) Oh, I'll bet that's the Italian (flag)...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We've got to get a distance.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, you're looking right at it, but it's only 10 meters away.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's hanging in front of the hills.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You're looking right at the flag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's hanging in front of the hills.
We thought we had an artifact (in the TV) or something like that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, bag 486 is a light-colored rock taken about 3 meters to the right of the Rover.
You should be able to pick it out in that last pan, unless the focus was bad.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to have you climb on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let's go to LCRU power off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, (pause) we better get going.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) You know, I don't think there is any subfloor in here.
The rocks are so dust covered that it's hard to be sure, but no rock I picked up looked like subfloor.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got three of them that time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you read me through the LM?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I hope they (the pictures) came out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I hope it (the seatbelt)'s untwisted this time, so I can get off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If old "twinkletoes" (Gene, himself) can do it.
Jack, there's a big one (a rock) right there, in my floor pan.
(Pause) That's what I did last time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The charge is off to the right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can clear it this way or...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause, thinking back to Houston's mistaken impression that the out-of focus flag was a background object) I bet you they thought there was some more orange soil over there on the hills.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get out of this block field, we'll be able to move it (that is, speed up) a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
